rationale.R1.6: The delegation targets a paragraph, making sentence-level flow, register, concision, and readability the central expected responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a vague request should preserve uncertainty about what kind of feedback was intended.
rationale.RX.2: The paragraph contains cautious empirical claims, so avoiding inflated interpretation remains active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must only project responsibilities and not actually review or revise the paragraph.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact discusses model performance, the projection should remain sensitive to possible downstream misuse of overstated results.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the paragraph-level artifact.
